title: Scope open-PR blockers to claimed repositories
goal: Keep Bureau's fail-closed open-PR and task-binding guards while ensuring a PR blocks only its own repository or the explicitly claimed repositories of its bound task, never unrelated repository balls.

acceptance.assertion: An open PR with missing, invalid or conflicting Bureau task binding blocks new write work in that PR's repository.
An unbound or invalid PR in one repository does not block unrelated repositories.
A valid task that explicitly claims multiple repositories blocks conflicting writes only across those claimed repositories.
what-now, repo-balls, conflicts and live-conflicts project the same repository-scoped blocker semantics.
The live state with Grabowski PRs #140/#145 and Lenskit PR #956 no longer produces unrelated Bureau, Cabinet, Chronik or Steuerboard blockers while same-repo blockers remain visible.

metadata.live_finding: Open PR blockers from Grabowski and Lenskit were projected onto unrelated repositories by live-conflicts.
validation: Bureau validate passed on Python 3.10 and 3.12.
Local make validate reported 473 passing tests, Ruff green, bridge policy green and Bureau check valid.
Live blocker projection fell from 10 repository findings to 3; unrelated Cabinet, Leitstand, Lenskit, Schauwerk, Steuerboard and Vibe-Lab balls no longer inherited foreign blockers.
Claim eligibility remains global while repository-local and cross-repository reasons are reported separately.

verified_acceptance.same-repo-fail-closed: Invalid or unbound PRs reserve their source repository and remain local blockers there.
verified_acceptance.multi-repo-claims: Valid bound tasks reserve explicit write-conflicting repository claims without relabeling global claim blockers as local.
verified_acceptance.projection-consistency: frontier, what-now, repo-balls and live-conflicts consume the separated local/cross-repository reasons.
verified_acceptance.live-regression-proof: Live conflicts retained only Bureau, Chronik and Grabowski findings in the cited observation.

metadata.outcome: Open-PR blockers remain fail-closed but are projected only onto their source repository or explicit conflicting repository claims.
